+## Parent
+
+Final step in the architectural redesign tracked by **01KQTC1VNQM9KC90S65P7QX9N1**.
+
+## Goal
+
+After the cutover (steps 10ŌĆō13) lands, the `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md` should reflect the post-redesign architecture exactly: stateless kernel over per-decision snapshots, consumer-owned scope registry. Each preceding step updated the README incrementally; this step audits the result and fixes any drift.
+
+## What to do
+
+### 1. Read the README cold
+
+Open `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md` and read it as if you'd never seen the project before. Note anything that:
+
+- References primitives that no longer exist (e.g. `register_scope`, `unregister_scope`, `update_rect`, `check_overlap_warning`, kernel-side `FocusScope` struct, `validated_layers`, `overlap_warn_partner`, `state.handle_unregister`)
+- Implies the kernel maintains a scope replica
+- Contradicts the snapshot-per-decision model
+- Has stale code examples or signatures
+- Has dangling "transitional" / "in transit" / "currently" language that should be cleaned up
+
+### 2. Verify against the actual code
+
+Walk the public surface of the crate after step 12 lands:
+
+```bash
+cargo doc -p swissarmyhammer-focus --no-deps --open
+```
+
+Cross-reference every exported type, method, and IPC command name in the README against what the crate actually exports. Anything in the README that's not in the code is stale; anything in the code that's prominent but missing from the README may need adding.
+
+### 3. Walk the consumer side too
+
+Check that the README's description of "what consumers do" matches what `kanban-app` actually does after step 13:
+
+- Consumer maintains `LayerScopeRegistry` per layer Ō£ō
+- Consumer reads `getBoundingClientRect()` per decision Ō£ō
+- Consumer calls `spatial_navigate(focused_fq, direction, snapshot)` Ō£ō
+- Consumer calls `spatial_focus(fq, snapshot)` on click / programmatic focus Ō£ō
+- Consumer calls `spatial_focus_lost(focused_fq, lost_parent_zone, lost_layer_fq, snapshot)` when the focused FQ unmounts Ō£ō
+- Consumer runs needless-nesting detection in dev mode Ō£ō
+- Consumer does NOT call register_scope / unregister_scope / update_rect (they don't exist anymore) Ō£ō
+
+If the README's narrative diverges from this, fix the README.
+
+### 4. Tighten the structure
+
+After all the incremental edits in steps 1-13, the README may have accreted overlapping / repetitive sections. Reorganize so a new reader gets:
+
+- Top: what the crate is (one paragraph). The kernel: layer stack + focus state machine + pathfinding/fallback. NOT a scope registry.
+- Primitives: `Layer`, `NavSnapshot`, `SnapshotScope`. (No kernel-side FocusScope; that's a consumer concept now.)
+- Operations: nav / focus / focus-lost / clear / push-layer / pop-layer. Each with its IPC signature.
+- The contract with consumers (one section): consumer owns scope structure + geometry; kernel owns focus state + pathfinding; per-decision snapshots are the only data exchange about scope shape.
+- Pathfinding rules (geometric beam pick) ŌĆö keep the existing math, it's good.
+- Fallback rules (the cascade) ŌĆö keep.
+
+### 5. Remove stale references in surrounding docs
+
+Check for stale references to deleted APIs in:
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` doc comment (the crate-level docs)
+- Any `//!` or `///` comments in the public-API modules
+- `MEMORY.md` entries about spatial-nav architecture (look in `~/.claude/projects/.../memory/`)
+
+These don't all have to be fixed in this step, but file follow-up tasks for any non-trivial cleanup.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- README reads cleanly cold to a reader who knows nothing about the project's history
+- Every type / method / IPC name in the README appears in the actual code
+- No "transitional" or "currently" language describing things that have already happened
+- No references to deleted APIs (register_scope, unregister_scope, update_rect, check_overlap_warning, etc.)
+- Consumer-contract section is clear about what each side owns
+- `cargo doc -p swissarmyhammer-focus --no-deps` produces clean docs that match the README
+
+## Files
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md` ŌĆö primary
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` ŌĆö crate-level doc comment review
+- Any module-level `//!` doc comments that need refresh
+- New follow-up tasks if substantial drift is found elsewhere
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Behavioral changes ŌĆö by this step the redesign is complete; this is documentation only
+- Memory-file cleanup beyond filing follow-up tasks (the user maintains those)
\ No newline at end of file
